data.name: HeroTitle
data.value: Turn large document collections into searchable, chat-ready knowledge.

data.name: HeroText
data.value: Harpyx is an open-source platform that helps teams transform scattered documents into structured, searchable knowledge. It gives organizations control over workspaces, permissions, hosting and AI model configuration, while letting users ask focused questions over the content of each project.

data.name: PreviewAnswer
data.value: Harpyx retrieves the most relevant document sections, keeps the answer tied to the selected project, and lets the configured model respond with the right context.

data.name: FeatureDocumentsText
data.value: Harpyx stores the original upload, extracts text, splits long content into searchable parts, and prepares each project for retrieval. It is built for collections that go beyond a handful of files.

data.name: FeatureAccessTitle
data.value: Separated by tenant, workspace and project

data.name: FeatureAccessText
data.value: Teams can keep customers, business units or internal groups isolated through tenants, workspaces, projects, roles and permissions.

data.name: FeatureModelsTitle
data.value: Bring the model setup that fits your instance

data.name: FeatureModelsText
data.value: Use configured hosted models, connect provider APIs, or expose local models from your own infrastructure when your deployment requires tighter control.

data.name: NotebookTitle
data.value: A more controllable document AI workspace

data.name: NotebookText1
data.value: Notebook-style tools are excellent for personal research, but many teams need something they can host, inspect, extend and govern. Harpyx keeps that workflow familiar while adding multi-tenant organization, project-level access and API-driven automation.

data.name: NotebookText2
data.value: The goal is practical: let users work with a virtually unlimited number of documents and document types, while operators keep control over storage, authentication, encryption, usage limits and model availability.

data.name: OpenSourceTitle
data.value: Built in the open for SaaS and self-hosted deployments

data.name: OpenSourceText
data.value: Harpyx is released under the MIT license. You can inspect the code, deploy your own instance, adapt the platform to your environment, or contribute improvements upstream.

data.name: InstallCardText
data.value: Run Harpyx with your own database, object storage, search engine, queue and authentication settings.

data.name: LicenseCardText
data.value: Use it, modify it and integrate it with commercial or internal systems according to the MIT license terms.
